ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a pork belly food product entry; the marinated and grilled preparation is not separately coded. Closest parent match used for the primary ingredient.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers grilled pig belly; the gochujang marinade component is not separately faceted. Closest parent code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Korean-style marinated grilled pork belly as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 180 g (one plate), providing around 550 kcal, 28 g protein, 45 g fat, and 8 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork, fresh, belly, raw (FDC ID 168291) and USDA SR Legacy — Pork, cured, bacon, cooked, pan-fried, adapted for marinated grilled preparation with gochujang
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for gochujang-marinated grilled pork belly exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central pork belly (raw, FDC ID 168291) adjusted for grilling cooking loss (~15% moisture reduction) and marinade contribution (gochujang, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, sugar) consistent with standard Korean yangnyeom samgyeopsal recipes. Input values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against the USDA adaptation and retained where consistent. Sodium is elevated relative to plain pork belly due to gochujang and soy sauce marinade. Values are flagged as estimated.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate based on mixed meat dish with fermented chilli marinade (low GI due to dominant protein and fat content with minimal carbohydrate from marinade)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for gochujang-marinated grilled pork belly. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous category for high-protein, high-fat meat dishes with minimal carbohydrate content. The marinade contributes approximately 4.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g, predominantly from gochujang and sugar; the dominant macronutrient profile (fat 25 g, protein 15.6 g) suppresses postprandial glucose response. GL per serving = (35 × 4.4 × 1.8) / 100 = 2.77, rounded to 2.8. This is consistent with peer-reviewed GI methodology for mixed protein-fat dishes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 527.8 mg per 100 g from gochujang and soy sauce marinade; saturated fat 10 g per 100 g contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100 g per meal and request reduced soy sauce and gochujang in the marinade, as a standard 180 g serving delivers approximately 950 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the 2400 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.4 g per 100 g) and low GI (~35) are favourable for glycaemic control, but high saturated fat (10 g per 100 g) worsens insulin resistance with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and high protein slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose response, making occasional consumption acceptable for individuals with type 2 diabetes; however, the high saturated fat load should prompt limiting frequency to no more than once or twice per week.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 10 g per 100 g and cholesterol 55.6 mg per 100 g from pork belly; sodium 527.8 mg per 100 g from marinade; trans fat 0.28 g per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as an occasional food only, limiting portions to 100 g or less, as a full 180 g serving provides 18 g saturated fat — 90% of the recommended daily limit — alongside elevated sodium and trace trans fat from the pork belly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat and arachidonic acid content in pork belly amplify TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; high sodium worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit this dish due to the pro-inflammatory saturated fat and arachidonic acid load from pork belly, and the high sodium from gochujang and soy sauce which exacerbates joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ajumma's OG Grilled Pork Belly is a Korean-style dish served in Singapore, featuring thick-cut pork belly (samgyeopsal) marinated in a spicy fermented chilli base — primarily gochujang — combined with soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and sugar. The preparation involves flash-frying followed by grilling, producing a caramelised, crispy exterior with a juicy, fatty interior. The dish is rich in fat (25 g per 100 g) and protein (15.6 g per 100 g), with moderate sodium (527.8 mg per 100 g) from the marinade. Saturated fat is high at 10 g per 100 g, making it a calorie-dense food at approximately 305 kcal per 100 g. Carbohydrate content is low at 4.4 g per 100 g, giving it a low estimated glycaemic index of approximately 35. A standard serving of 180 g provides around 550 kcal. Clinically, the dish is high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat, and moderate concern for type 2 diabetes due to insulin-resistance risk from regular high saturated fat intake despite the low carbohydrate profile. The dish is culturally rooted in Korean barbecue tradition (samgyeopsal gui) and is popular in Korean restaurants across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for gochujang-marinated grilled pork belly exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central pork belly (raw) adjusted for grilling and marinade contribution. The input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7; values were cross-checked against the USDA adaptation and are internally consistent. Medium confidence is appropriate for an adapted estimate.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for gochujang-marinated grilled pork belly as a composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026. The input source record cited foodo.sg but the URL provided (https://foodo.sg/) does not correspond to an HPB-published nutrient composition entry for this specific dish; the HPB source was therefore not used as primary nutrient evidence. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central pork belly (FDC ID 168291) with marinade adjustment. GI is an analogous estimate (low, ~35) based on University of Sydney GI Database methodology for high-protein, high-fat meat dishes with minimal carbohydrate. Ethnic tags 'Bibimbap' and 'Korean Fried Chicken' in the input were removed as they are unrelated dish names, not tags applicable to this record. The dish is not halal-certified by default as pork is the primary ingredient.
